Topic 2: Iran update

[191:56]I'm sure we'll be talking about it tomorrow as well.

[192:00]There have been some new developments in the ongoing crisis in the Middle East right now.

[192:07]And related to the state of the union,

[192:09]many people believe that Trump was going to address

[192:12]the crisis in the Middle East during his speech.

[192:15]And there were even rumors that Trump might declare war

[192:18]during the speech, that he might make the case for a war,

[192:22]that maybe he might announce a new ultimatum

[192:24]or a military action for that matter.

[192:27]And what is interesting is that Trump

[192:29]really did not touch the issue at all.

[192:33]He briefly discussed Iran and simply said that Iran will never be able to acquire a nuclear weapon,

[192:40]which is not really

[192:41]any kind of addition to the conversation.

[192:45]Now, the reason this is notable is because tomorrow, actually in a matter of hours, is going to be the third round of talks between the U.S.

[192:54]and Iran in Switzerland.

[192:56]We are also coming up on the deadline that Trump set to make a deal.

[193:01]So this might be make or break.

[193:04]Trump said last Tuesday that Iran had 10 days, maybe up to two weeks to make a deal.

[193:12]Well, we're coming up on 10 days.

[193:15]It'll be 10 days on Friday.

[193:17]It'll be two weeks on Tuesday.

[193:20]So it looks like everything is riding on this meeting.

[193:29]And if there is, it's gonna have to happen this weekend.

[193:37]with Iran.

[193:39]There have been some developments on this front.

[193:42]The first one being, and this is maybe one of the more pessimistic developments, the

[193:50]Trump administration said in the last round of talks, which was last week, they said that

[193:57]had a window to meet us where we are. It's like I said on Monday, this is not really

[194:03]a negotiation. These are really more just discussions. And the reason I say that is because the United

[194:09]States has made their position very clear and they have a red line. The US position

[194:14]is we're not going to ask you to give up your missiles. We're not going to ask you to give

[194:21]but you do have to give up your nuclear enrichment.

[194:24]You have to have a zero enrichment capacity.

[194:28]And that they have not budged on for a very long time.

[194:32]So they said this on Thursday.

[194:34]And the way that the White House talked about it

[194:36]is they said, you've got about another week

[194:40]to close the gap between your position and our position.

[194:43]That doesn't mean we're gonna meet in the middle.

[194:45]That doesn't mean we're each gonna give concessions.

[194:48]It means this is an ultimatum and you are either going to surrender and capitulate to

[194:54]our demands or we're going to war.

[194:55]So make up your mind, tell us what it's going to be.

[194:58]Are you going to give up or are we going to war?

[195:00]And so the administration said on Thursday that Iran had another week, there'd be another

[195:05]round of talks, but this is really where we are.

[195:09]So the Omani representative said, we will deliver Iran's response on Tuesday, which

[195:18]and the US has received Iran's response.

[195:21]And according to Israeli media,

[195:23]Iran is not budging on enrichment.

[195:26]Now they wanna give concessions

[195:28]on their stockpile of highly enriched uranium.

[195:31]And they may concede that they will enrich uranium

[195:34]at a low level for a short amount of time.

[195:38]But according to all the information we have,

[195:41]Iran's position has not changed since last week.
